2604.13959.title: [Emerging Ideas] Artificial Tripartite Intelligence: A Bio-Inspired, Sensor-First Architecture for Physical AI
2604.13959.paper_summary: As AI moves from data centers to robots and wearables, scaling ever-larger models becomes insufficient. Physical AI operates under tight latency, energy, privacy, and reliability constraints, and its performance depends not only on model capacity but also on how signals are acquired through controllable sensors in dynamic environments. We present Artificial Tripartite Intelligence (ATI), a bio-inspired, sensor-first architectural contract for physical AI. ATI is tripartite at the systems level: a Brainstem (L1) provides reflexive safety and signal-integrity control, a Cerebellum (L2) performs continuous sensor calibration, and a Cerebral Inference Subsystem spanning L3/L4 supports routine skill selection and execution, coordination, and deep reasoning. This modular organization allows sensor control, adaptive sensing, edge-cloud execution, and foundation model reasoning to co-evolve within one closed-loop architecture, while keeping time-critical sensing and control on device and invoking higher-level inference only when needed. We instantiate ATI in a mobile camera prototype under dynamic lighting and motion. In our routed evaluation (L3-L4 split inference), compared to the default auto-exposure setting, ATI (L1/L2 adaptive sensing) improves end-to-end accuracy from 53.8% to 88% while reducing remote L4 invocations by 43.3%. These results show the value of co-designing sensing and inference for embodied AI.
2604.13959.paper_summary_zh: 隨著人工智慧從資料中心移動到機器人和可穿戴設備，僅僅擴展更大的模型已經不夠。物理人工智慧在延遲、能量、隱私和可靠性方面受到嚴格的限制，其性能不僅取決於模型容量，還取決於如何通過可控傳感器在動態環境中獲取信號。我們提出了人工三元智能（ATI），這是一種受生物啟發的、以傳感器為首的物理人工智慧架構契約。ATI 在系統層面上是三元的：腦幹（L1）提供反射安全性和信號完整性控制，小腦（L2）進行持續的傳感器校準，而跨越 L3/L4 的大腦推理子系統支持例行技能選擇和執行、協調以及深度推理。這種模組化組織使得傳感器控制、自適應感知、邊緣雲執行和基礎模型推理能在一個封閉循環架構中共同演化，同時將時間關鍵的感知和控制保持在設備上，僅在需要時調用更高層次的推理。我們在動態照明和運動下的移動相機原型中實現了 ATI。在我們的路由評估（L3-L4 分割推理）中，與默認自動曝光設置相比，ATI（L1/L2 自適應感知）將端到端準確性從 53.8% 提高到 88%，同時將遠程 L4 調用減少了 43.3%。這些結果顯示了為具身人工智慧共同設計感知和推理的價值。
2604.13959.author: You Rim Choi et.al.
2604.13959.authors: You Rim Choi, Subeom Park, Hyung-Sin Kim
